OatFi Portfolio Review

OatFi — Portfolio Review

As of June 25, 2026  ·  analytics_development.mart  ·  DPD: cutoffdate − mat_dt, curr_upb > 0
Generated 2026-06-25 14:46
Excl: Balance Payments, Oat Financial LLC
Assessment
Claude · auto-generated

# Portfolio Delinquency Review – Q2 2026

Portfolio delinquency improved modestly in the quarter. Order's 30–60 DPD bucket declined 21 basis points QoQ to 0.54%, with meaningful month-over-month improvement of 47 basis points, while 60–90 and 90–120 buckets remain stable. Koverly Inc. remains current across all cohorts. Lawtrades carries structural payment delay characteristics typical of its partner model but shows improvement in the 30–60 cohort, declining 30.62 percentage points QoQ. Flychain entered the 90–120 DPD bucket at 7.19%, up from zero last quarter, warranting monitoring. Tab Commerce and BRKR.IO remain manageable, each below 1% in most buckets.

Leading indicators reveal material stress ahead. Order carries $8.9M in leading balances, with confirmed delinquency of $908K already materializing; this concentration represents 28% of Order's total portfolio. Lawtrades has $1.4M in leading obligation balances, with only $76K confirmed, indicating a 94% pipeline of future delinquencies concentrated in Ironclad ($224.6K across three loans, 42–53 days past partner due date) and Apollo.io ($83.4K, 53 days). BRKR.IO shows $377K leading against modest current UPB of $1M. Cledara's $152K leading balance stands against zero confirmed delinquency, a reversal signal to monitor.

Construction and Admin/Support sectors are deteriorating. Construction carries $1.2M UPB with 20.97% delinquency (31+ DPD), while Admin/Support shows the highest stress at 22.78% delinquency across $993K. Real Estate mirrors Construction at 20.95% delinquency. Professional Services deteriorates to 11.65% delinquency, primarily driven by Lawtrades' obligor mix. Retail segments split: one at 4.44% and a second at 9.11%, suggesting partner-driven segmentation rather than macro pressure. Health Care remains resilient at 5.76%. These patterns reflect both obligor concentration within partners and underlying sector cyclicality, though Construction and Admin/Support stress appears obligor-specific rather than economy-wide.

Large delinquent names concentrate in Order and Relay partners. Order's three largest 30+ DPD balances are HPI Canna Inc. ($246.5K), Columbia Care ($215.7K), and 14th Round Inc. ($104.4K)—all cannabis and lifestyle retail, reflecting sector stress overlapping with Order's obligor concentration. Koverly's largest delinquent name is Marino Ave Inc. at $184.7K (construction materials). BRKR.IO's Venus Brands at $127.5K (manufacturing) represents 12.6% of that partner's UPB. Flychain's two $100K delinquencies (Camelot Chateau and Noble House) are both senior care facilities, indicating sector clustering. Leading obligors show Havenly Inc. (Order) replicated four times at $52.9K each (158 DPD days), suggesting data integrity or syndication issues; three Ironclad loans (Lawtrades) total $224.6K at 42–53 days past due.

Charge-offs over the trailing 12 months total $4.7M across nine partners. Koverly leads at $1.75M, followed by Avalara at $1.22M and Order at $1.0M; these three account for 71% of realized losses. Flychain contributed $459K and Tab Commerce $165K. Lawtrades' charge-off volume remains modest at $110K despite current delinquency patterns. No clear seasonal pattern emerges, but the concentration within three partners—all of which carry elevated current delinquency—suggests portfolio-level obligor selection or underwriting consistency issues that pre-date 2026.

30+ DPD: —
Cohort loss rate trend — trailing 3 / 6 / 12 matured-cohort loss % (dollar-weighted)

Each point = trailing N fully matured origination cohorts aggregated as total 120+ DPD losses ÷ total originated. A cohort is matured once its latest-maturing loan has cleared the 120-day loss window, so the series ends before today; more recent cohorts are still developing losses and are excluded.

Max possible loss by cohort — 30+ DPD ceiling vs booked 120+ loss (short lag)

Every eventual 120+ loss first passes through 30 DPD, so a cohort’s current 30+ DPD balance plus already-booked losses is the ceiling on what it can lose — observable ~120 days after origination, about 3 months sooner than confirmed loss. Bars = originated ($K). Red = max possible loss % (ceiling). Black dashed = booked 120+ loss %. The gap is loans still in flight; it closes as the cohort matures.

Loss % by origination cohort — cumulative losses ÷ originated ($K stacked bars, loss % line)

Bars = originated ($K) per cohort month. Lines on right axis = % of that cohort’s originated balance currently 30–60 / 60–90 / 90–120 DPD (amber/orange/red) and cumulative loss % (black). Recent cohorts understated as losses accrue over time.

Loss by underwriting vintage — initial line assigned vs 120+ DPD balance as % of line

X-axis = month of initial underwrite decision. Bars = total credit line assigned at first underwrite ($K). Red line = cumulative loss % of line assigned (right axis). Recent vintages understated as losses take time to develop.

Loss by underwriting vintage — total borrowed vs 120+ DPD balance as % of total borrowed

X-axis = month of initial underwrite decision. Bars = total amount actually borrowed (sum of original_amount across all loans) by orgs in each vintage cohort ($K). Red line = cumulative 120+ DPD balance as % of total borrowed (right axis). Denominator is realized borrowing, not credit line capacity. Recent vintages understated.

Loss ratio by origination cohort — cumulative 120+ DPD losses ÷ cumulative income collected

Bars = income collected from each cohort’s loans ($K): interest + fees + late fees + non-accrual items. Red line = cumulative loss % of cumulative income (right axis). Dashed orange line = monthly loss ratio (non-cumulative). Recent cohorts understated as both losses and income continue to accrue.

Monthly charge-offs ($K principal)
OatFi Credit Risk  ·  DPD methodology: cutoffdate − mat_dt, curr_upb > 0. Leading indicator: past partner due_date, not yet past OatFi mat_dt. Loss rate: first 120 DPD crossing or chargeoff ÷ trailing 12m originations × 12. Cohort loss %: 120+ DPD losses ÷ originated, matured cohorts only (latest loan past maturity + 120d). Utilization >120% excluded. Partners and orgs under $10K excluded.
